summary: Split the former monolithic fighter source `src/melee/ft/ft_0D14.c` and header into many smaller common-action files and headers, updated the build configuration and GALE01 splits/symbols to match the new object boundaries, and marked completed pieces as linked/matching where possible. The PR removed `ft_0D14.c` and `ft_0D14.h`, added action-focused files such as Kinoko start/end, ItemScrew, DamageScrew, Landing, Squat, Attack100, CaptureCut, and Throw units, and replaced broad `ft/ft_0D14.h` includes across fighter code with narrower `ftCommon/...` headers. The decomp.dev bot reported +0.27% linked code, +10572 bytes, and 168 new matches, balanced by the same functions moving out of the old `ft_0D14` unit.
what_changed: Deleted the old aggregate `src/melee/ft/ft_0D14.c` source, which had 2390 deleted lines, and deleted `src/melee/ft/ft_0D14.h`, which had 327 deleted lines.

Introduced separate source/header units for many common fighter actions previously grouped under `ft_0D14`, including mushroom size effects, screw/item screw states, landing, squat/squat wait/squat reverse, rapid jab/Attack100, capture cut, and throw.

Updated `config/GALE01/splits.txt` so the old `melee/ft/ft_0D14.c` text/data/sdata/sdata2 ranges were divided into per-file ranges from `0x800D14E4` through `0x800DE3FC`, with separate data and small-data ranges where needed.

Updated `configure.py` to replace the single `Object(NonMatching, "melee/ft/ft_0D14.c")` entry with multiple object entries. Several new units are marked `Matching`, while `ft_0D31.c`, `ftCo_Attack100.c`, `ftCo_CaptureCut.c`, and `ftCo_Throw.c` remain `NonMatching` in the excerpt.
Replaced many includes of `ft/ft_0D14.h` with narrower action-specific headers such as `ftCommon/ftCo_Attack100.h`, `ftCommon/ftCo_Landing.h`, `ftCommon/ftCo_Throw.h`, and the Kinoko headers.

Adjusted `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` around the split ranges, including localizing some function symbol scopes such as `ftCo_Squat_Enter` and `ftCo_SquatRv_Enter`, and renaming many local constants/objects from address-style `ftCo_804D...` labels to compiler-style local labels like `@213`, `@227`, and `lbl_803C6FF0$229`.

Updated dependent files such as `src/melee/db/dbanim.c`, Captain/Fox/Luigi/GameWatch/Mewtwo/Ness/Sheik/common action sources, `fighter.c`, and `ftdata.c` to include the new headers needed after the split.
smart_moves: The split preserved the exact section ordering and addresses in `splits.txt`, rather than only moving C code, which is critical when turning one linked object range into many smaller objects.
The build list in `configure.py` was granular: already-completed segments were linked as `Matching`, while still-problematic segments were left `NonMatching` instead of overclaiming progress.
The PR narrowed header dependencies by replacing `ft_0D14.h` includes with action-specific `ftCommon` headers, making future ownership and include hygiene clearer.
Symbol-map updates reflected the new object boundaries, including local scopes and local constant names, reducing ambiguity after splitting static/local data across multiple translation units.
The decomp.dev report shows the apparent broken matches were relocations from `main/melee/ft/ft_0D14` into new units, while linked code still increased by +10572 bytes.
decomp_lessons: When splitting a large decomp unit, update source files, headers, build object declarations, `splits.txt`, and `symbols.txt` together; a source-only move is insufficient for matching/linking progress.

Use action/state names for new common fighter files where the behavior is known, e.g. `ftCo_Landing`, `ftCo_Squat`, `ftCo_Attack100`, `ftCo_CaptureCut`, and `ftCo_Throw`, while preserving anonymous address-style files such as `ft_0D27` and `ft_0D31` where naming evidence remains weak.
A decomp.dev report after a split can show equal numbers of new and broken matches because functions moved object units; interpret this as a relocation unless total linked code regresses.
Keep partially matched or difficult units `NonMatching` after a split. Smaller object files can still improve reviewability and future matching even before every function in the range matches.
After removing a catch-all header, audit every consumer and include only the specific new headers that provide the referenced declarations.
naming_conventions: Common fighter action files use the `ftCo_` prefix under `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/`, with CamelCase action suffixes such as `ftCo_KinokoGiantStart`, `ftCo_KinokoSmallEnd`, `ftCo_ItemScrew`, `ftCo_DamageScrew`, `ftCo_Landing`, `ftCo_SquatWait`, `ftCo_Attack100`, `ftCo_CaptureCut`, and `ftCo_Throw`.
Headers mirror their source filenames, e.g. `ftCo_Attack100.c` has `ftCo_Attack100.h`, and consuming files include them as `"ftCommon/ftCo_Attack100.h"`.
Unknown or insufficiently named ranges can remain address-based, as with `ft_0D27.c` and `ft_0D31.c`, rather than forcing speculative semantic names.
Local constants and static data in `symbols.txt` may use compiler-like local labels such as `@199`, `@213`, or suffixed local labels like `lbl_803C6FF0$229` after object splitting.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted one large nonmatching object into many smaller objects in `configure.py`, enabling matching granularity by file instead of by the entire `ft_0D14` range.
Maintained exact text/data/sdata/sdata2 boundaries in `config/GALE01/splits.txt`, including gaps/alignment-sensitive small-data ranges such as `0x804D8F68` through `0x804D9110`.
Marked completed new units as `Object(Matching, ...)` while leaving `ft_0D31`, `ftCo_Attack100`, `ftCo_CaptureCut`, and `ftCo_Throw` as `Object(NonMatching, ...)` in the shown configure diff.
Adjusted symbol scopes to local where appropriate after splitting, shown by `ftCo_Squat_Enter` and `ftCo_SquatRv_Enter` gaining `scope:local`.
Used per-action headers to resolve cross-translation-unit references after removing the old aggregate `ft_0D14.h`.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 linked code at 26.55%, an increase of +0.27% and +10572 bytes.
The bot reported 168 new matches in the new split units and 168 broken matches in the old `main/melee/ft/ft_0D14` unit, consistent with functions moving from the deleted object into new objects.
The bot also reported 6 improvements in unmatched functions in new units and 6 corresponding regressions in the old removed unit.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that match or rename functions remaining nonmatching in `ftCo_Attack100.c`, `ftCo_CaptureCut.c`, `ftCo_Throw.c`, or `ft_0D31.c`.
Search for later removal or semantic renaming of address-based files `ft_0D27.c` and `ft_0D31.c`.
Check whether later PRs further narrow includes introduced by removing `ft_0D14.h`.
Look for follow-up symbol cleanup around local constants in the `0x804D8F68` to `0x804D9110` sdata2 range.
